articles.title: Israel strikes Beirut's southern suburbs, killing scores, days after ceasefire deal
articles.summary: Israel struck Beirut's southern suburbs without prior warning on Sunday, days after a US-supported ceasefire took effect and despite a US request not to hit the Lebanese capital. The IDF said it targeted a Hezbollah headquarters after the group fired two rockets at northern Israel earlier in the day. Lebanese authorities reported at least 92 people killed and more than 740 wounded in the strike. Netanyahu convened his defence chiefs for a situational assessment as Iran threatened to respond, with parliament speaker Mohammad Baqer Ghalibaf calling US bases and Israeli assets in the region 'legitimate targets.'

articles.title: Israeli forces kill nine in Gaza as Hamas and mediators renew ceasefire talks in Cairo
articles.summary: The Israeli army killed at least nine people and wounded dozens across the Gaza Strip since dawn on Sunday, including eight in a drone strike on a tent camp for displaced people in Gaza City. The attacks came as Hamas convened meetings with Egyptian mediators and other Palestinian factions in Cairo to salvage the so-called ceasefire that has been in place since October 2025. Hamas said the talks would focus on full implementation of the agreement's first phase, including ending Israeli violations, reopening border crossings and allowing humanitarian aid into the enclave.

articles.title: One killed, five wounded in shooting and ramming attack in Sharon region
articles.summary: One person was killed and at least five others wounded in a terror attack in central Israel's Sharon region on Sunday morning. The attack began near Kochav Yair by Kalkilya and continued past Tsur Yitzhak, Tzur Natan and Sde Eliyahu. Israeli police shot and killed one of the assailants, identified as an Israeli Arab from Tayibe, and searched for possible additional suspects. Netanyahu conducted a situational assessment following the attack.

articles.title: Netanyahu says Israel now controls 60% of Gaza and 'will soon reach 70%'
articles.summary: Opening Sunday's cabinet meeting, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Israel currently holds 60 percent of Gaza's territory and 'will soon reach 70 percent.' Rights groups and Palestinian officials say the expanding Israeli control contradicts the terms of the October 2025 ceasefire, which has been repeatedly strained by continued strikes. Israel has also been documented constructing earthen barriers inside the territory since the truce began.

articles.title: US-Iran ceasefire deal in jeopardy as Beirut strike tests fragile truce
articles.summary: A tentative US-Iran agreement reached last week to extend their ceasefire by 60 days and reopen nuclear talks came under fresh strain after Israel's strike on Beirut. The memorandum's broad strokes include a halt in violence, a ban on Iranian tolls in the Strait of Hormuz and the removal of mines from the waterway within 30 days, with possible sanctions waivers tied to progress. Iran's foreign ministry accused US forces of an earlier ceasefire violation near Hormuz, and Tehran has signalled its response to the Beirut attack could derail the diplomacy. Pakistan is reported to be trying to help restart talks between Tehran and Washington.

articles.title: Jordan's King Abdullah urges intensified efforts to strengthen US-Iran ceasefire
articles.summary: Jordan's King Abdullah II stressed the need to intensify efforts to bolster the ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran in talks touching on regional security. The Iraqi parliament speaker separately discussed regional security and cooperation with the Jordanian king and prime minister. The diplomacy comes as Gulf and Arab states press to prevent the fragile truces in Lebanon, Gaza and the Gulf from collapsing back into wider war.

articles.title: Seven OPEC+ producers agree modest July output increase
articles.summary: Seven OPEC+ producers, including Saudi Arabia, agreed to a modest increase in oil output for July as the group continues a gradual unwinding of earlier production cuts amid volatile prices linked to Gulf tensions. Separately, Saudi Arabia marked World Environment Day by highlighting biodiversity and sustainability projects, and the Saudi-funded Masam project reported clearing 798 explosive devices in Yemen.

articles.title: Israeli settlers torch Palestinian vehicles and property south of Nablus
articles.summary: Israeli settlers set fire to Palestinian vehicles and property during an attack on several areas south of Nablus in the occupied West Bank, as Israeli forces carried out raids and arrests across multiple locations. Rights monitors describe a continued surge in settler violence and military incursions across the West Bank, including armoured operations reported in the Balata refugee camp east of Nablus.

articles.title: Israeli military police investigate killing of seven-month-old Palestinian infant in Hebron
articles.summary: Israel's Military Police opened an investigation into the killing of seven-month-old Sam Fahd Abou Haikal, who died after a soldier fired at the family's car in Hebron, reportedly perceiving it as accelerating toward troops. The infant's parents were lightly wounded. The case adds to scrutiny of Israeli military conduct in the occupied West Bank, where rights groups have documented frequent civilian casualties.

articles.title: Syria's private-sector dialogue continues in Damascus as government seeks economic lifeline
articles.summary: Talks between Syria's transitional government and private-sector representatives continued in Damascus as authorities under President Ahmad al-Sharaa search for ways to revive a war-battered economy and attract investment. The discussions come amid ongoing security challenges, including a reported Damascus security operation in Suwayda and continued Israeli activity in southern Syria.

articles.title: US weighs response as Middle East ceasefires fray on multiple fronts
articles.summary: The Trump administration faced mounting pressure to keep its diplomacy on track as ceasefires in Lebanon, Gaza and the Gulf all came under strain within a single weekend. Israel's strike on Beirut, despite a US request to hold off, exposed friction between Washington and Netanyahu, while Iran weighed retaliation that could collapse the tentative nuclear-talks framework. Analysts warn the overlapping truces remain dangerously reversible.
